name: 28 · Turtle Drive (ROS)
description:
End-to-end ROS-backend demo against a turtlesim robot reachable over rosbridge. A four-step net drives the toy turtle and reads back where it ended up, exercising all three core ROS operations against one runner-local rosbridge endpoint:

  Start → teleport (call_service /turtle1/teleport_absolute, turtlesim/srv/TeleportAbsolute, {x:1.0,y:1.0,theta:0.0}) → drive (publish_topic /turtle1/cmd_vel, geometry_msgs/Twist, linear.x=2.0) → read_pose (await_topic /turtle1/pose, turtlesim/Pose) → End.

Each ROS AutomatedStep is deployed to the `ros_fleet` presence pool (seeded from demos/resources/ros_fleet.json) AND declares the placement Requirement `ros.robot_model == turtlesim` over the `ros` capability type (seeded from demos/capability-types/ros.json). At claim time the compiler injects the Requirement into the claim payload; the presence pool's `t_grant` guard admits ONLY a runner whose advertised caps satisfy it. The ROS connection is runner-local — the runner advertises a reachable rosbridge and its executor daemon is configured with the WebSocket URL (`EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL`); there is no workspace resource binding.

The `await_topic` step derives its output port from the `turtlesim/Pose` message type, so the pose fields land at the TOP LEVEL of read_pose's output (`x`, `y`, `theta`, `linear_velocity`, `angular_velocity`). End maps `final_pose_x = {{ read_pose.x }}`, `final_pose_y = {{ read_pose.y }}`, `final_theta = {{ read_pose.theta }}` onto the instance result.

LIVE ONLY: this demo can only be RUN after `just dev ros-up`, which builds the turtlesim+rosbridge container, runs it on a slot-aware host port, and enrolls a `ros_fleet` runner advertising `ros.robot_model=turtlesim, ros.ros_distro=jazzy` whose executor points `EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL` at that bridge. Without ros-up there is no capacity and the instance parks at the first claim. After the teleport+drive, the awaited pose is near (1,1) advanced along +x, so `final_pose_x >= 1.0`.
